author: jsign
content:
<@944007601400741989> , these last days I was talking with <@755913785620824175>  (EF testing team) on how we could have a [Hive dashboard](https://hive.ethpandaops.io/#/) for the [zkevm spec tests](https://github.com/ethereum/execution-spec-tests/releases?q=zkevm&expanded=true). It would be helpful to have a public dashboard to get a sense which ELs are passing hte tests.

While they are runnable today via t8n in ELs, this tool isn’t widely available in all ELs plus isn’t the main medium used in Hive.

Hive is a way to run EEST tests through the EngineAPI which is a nicier API for ELs since they have to implement it for mainnet functioning. 

I added support for generating the tests also in `blockchain_tests_engine` format, which is the ones already used in Hive for runs.

While thinking about this, I thought it might be a good moment to also push for requiring your execution-api proposal for newPayloadWithWitness, given we need an EngineAPI way of running the test payloads returning the witness to compare against the expected ones.

Spencer already created a vibe-PR to add support for `consume engine` of this new endpoint (and have a fallback). That means that [in Hive](https://github.com/ethpandaops/hive) we can configure using this new `consume engine` support to run the fixtures.

I think given you’ve an Ethrex-PoC of the new REST+SSZ, it could become a good first candidate to try this idea out. LMK if you’re interested in this line of work,  if not I’ll probably making progress on this in the next days. :)  (just pinging since feels like a good first use case to gain more traction on the new engineapi proposal).
